flows.name: develop-feature
flows.description: Develop a feature in a git worktree: pick or create a worktree (branch name = worktree name), implement, test, screenshot, commit, and open a PR.

steps.name: Select Worktree
steps.content:
# SELECT WORKTREE

## PURPOSE

List all existing git worktrees and ask the user which one to use, or whether to create a new one.

## WORKFLOW

1. Run `git worktree list` to get all existing worktrees.
2. Write to `{{step.dir}}/hitl.md`:
   - List the existing worktrees (path and branch for each).
   - Ask the user to either type the name of an existing worktree to continue work there, or provide a new short name (e.g. `feat-my-feature`) to create a fresh one. The name will be used as both the worktree directory name and the git branch name.
3. Write to `{{step.dir}}/_result.md`: the full output of `git worktree list` for subsequent steps.

steps.name: Research and Implement
steps.content:
# RESEARCH AND IMPLEMENT

## PURPOSE

Research the codebase, implement the feature `{{flow.TASK_DESCRIPTION}}`, run tests, and take a screenshot of the new feature.

## WORKFLOW

### 1. Set up the worktree

The user's chosen worktree name is `{{hitl.response.0}}`.

- If `{{hitl.response.0}}` is an absolute path that already exists, use it as-is.
- If it is a short name (no slashes), check `.worktrees/{{hitl.response.0}}`:
  - If it exists, use it.
  - If it does not exist, create it: `git worktree add .worktrees/{{hitl.response.0}} -b {{hitl.response.0}}`

Save the absolute path of the worktree to `{{step.dir}}/worktree-path.txt` (one line, no trailing newline).

Run `git diff` and `git status` inside the worktree to understand its current state.

### 2. Set up an isolated Python environment

All commands from this point forward run inside the worktree directory.

- Create a venv if it does not already exist: `python -m venv .venv`
- Install the project and dev deps: `.venv/bin/pip install -e '.[dev]' -q`

### 3. Research and implement

- Explore the codebase to understand where changes are needed.
- Select the best implementation approach. Document your reasoning in `{{step.dir}}/_result.md`.
- Implement the feature. All code changes must stay inside the worktree.
- Write unit tests for the new code.

### 4. Run tests

From inside the worktree: `.venv/bin/pytest`

All tests must pass before continuing.

### 5. Screenshot the new feature

First, clean up any leftover UI or daemon from a previous attempt:

```bash
lsof -ti tcp:8899 2>/dev/null | xargs kill -15 2>/dev/null || true
LLMFLOWS_HOME=~/.llmflows-worktree-{{hitl.response.0}} .venv/bin/llmflows daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1
```

Start the UI with full isolation — separate data directory, port 8899, separate daemon:

```bash
export LLMFLOWS_HOME=~/.llmflows-worktree-{{hitl.response.0}}
.venv/bin/llmflows register --name worktree-{{hitl.response.0}}
.venv/bin/llmflows ui --port 8899 &
UI_PID=$!
sleep 5
```

Capture a screenshot using Playwright (install if needed):

```bash
.venv/bin/pip install playwright -q
.venv/bin/playwright install chromium --with-deps -q
.venv/bin/python -m playwright screenshot http://localhost:8899 {{attachment.dir}}/screenshot.png --wait-until networkidle
```

Navigate to the relevant route if the feature is not on the home page. Take additional screenshots as needed.

Leave the UI and daemon running — they will be cleaned up at the start of the next run.

### 6. Write summary

Write to `{{step.dir}}/_result.md`:
- Approach rationale
- Full list of changed files with brief description of each change
- Full test output

## FORBIDDEN

- Do not commit or push.
- Do not make changes outside the worktree.
- Do not touch the production daemon (`llmflows daemon start/stop` without `LLMFLOWS_HOME` set).

gates.message: worktree-path.txt must exist with the absolute path to the worktree.
Dependency installation inside the worktree venv must succeed.
All tests must pass inside the worktree.
At least one screenshot must be saved to the attachments directory as a .png file.

steps.name: Commit and PR
steps.content:
# COMMIT AND PR

## PURPOSE

Commit the implemented changes, push the branch, then create a new PR or add a progress comment to an existing one. Screenshots are delivered via Telegram only — do not commit them.

## WORKFLOW

### 1. Navigate to the worktree

The worktree path is in the context from the previous step (`worktree-path.txt`). Read it and navigate there.

Save the worktree path to `{{step.dir}}/worktree-path.txt` as well (for gate access).

### 2. Commit

Stage only source code changes — do not stage `.screenshots/`, `.venv/`, or any other generated files:

```bash
git add -A
git reset HEAD .screenshots/ 2>/dev/null || true
git commit -m "feat: {{flow.TASK_DESCRIPTION}}"
```

If there is nothing to commit (resuming after a previous run), skip the commit.

### 3. Push

```bash
BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
git push -u origin "$BRANCH"
```

### 4. Build the PR/comment body

```
## Summary
<one-paragraph summary of what was implemented and why this approach>

## Changes
<file tree with one-line description per changed file>

## Testing
<test results: pass count, any notable coverage>
```

### 5. Create PR or comment on existing

Check whether a PR already exists for this branch:

```bash
PR_URL=$(gh pr view --json url -q .url 2>/dev/null || true)
```

- **No existing PR** — create one:
  ```bash
  PR_URL=$(gh pr create \
    --title "feat: {{flow.TASK_DESCRIPTION}}" \
    --body "$BODY" \
    --base main)
  ```

- **Existing PR** — add a progress comment with the same body:
  ```bash
  gh pr comment "$PR_URL" --body "$BODY"
  ```

Save the PR URL to `{{step.dir}}/pr-url.txt` (one line).

### 6. Write inbox notification

Write to `{{run.dir}}/inbox.md` (the Telegram/Slack notification — screenshots are attached automatically from the attachments directory):
- PR URL as a clickable link
- One-paragraph summary of what was implemented
- File tree of changed files with a one-line description of each change
- Test results (pass count)

## FORBIDDEN

- Do not commit `.screenshots/`, `.venv/`, or any non-source files.
- Do not push to `main` or `master`.
- Do not merge the PR.
- Do not delete the worktree.

gates.message: pr-url.txt must exist with the GitHub PR URL.
pr-url.txt must contain a valid GitHub URL.
inbox.md must exist at the run directory root with the PR link and summary.
